concerns.text: T019 (geospatial matching) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but explicitly depends on T018 (ERA5 fetching). The task description states 'This task depends on T018 completing,' yet the [P] tag implies it can run in parallel with other tasks. This creates a hidden ordering violation if a parallel runner attempts T019 before T018 finishes.

T020 (time-based interpolation) is marked [P] but depends on T019 (geospatial matching) as stated in its description. Marking a task that consumes the output of a previous task as parallel-safe violates the producer-consumer dependency flow.

T026 (quadratic term) and T027 (spline basis) are both marked [P] and listed as independent tasks. However, the plan.md specifies a unified 'Non-Linearity Test Consumer Step' that computes AIC/BIC and a Wald test for the *joint* significance of linear and quadratic terms. Splitting these into two parallel tasks that save to different files (`nonlinearity_quadratic_comparison.csv` vs `nonlinearity_spline_comparison.csv`) breaks the semantic dependency required to perform the joint comparison and LRT against a single linear baseline.

T041 (extract cluster-robust variance) depends on the output of T032 (export model results). T041 is not marked [P], but T032 is not explicitly marked as a prerequisite in the task description, creating a potential race condition if T041 is scheduled before T032 completes in a parallel execution context.

Phase 0 tasks (T001-T006) are all marked [P]. While they are independent of each other, T006 (Write validation log) logically depends on the results of T002-T005. Marking T006 as [P] suggests it can run before the validation checks are complete, which is semantically incorrect.
